old_claim.note: Early Navigation is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim_absent: No direct edge remains because the scoped early_navigation node covers coastal and open-water navigation, not land route following by pack animals.

source_shape.source_locator: The overview defines pack animals by carrying goods or materials on their backs, rather than by nautical route-finding or open-water navigation.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Pack animal transport is about load-carrying working animals; coastal/open-water navigation is a separate transport-navigation technology.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source supports the pack-transport scope without implying a direct dependency on water-navigation methods.

ontology_before: Pack Animal Transport depended directly on early_navigation, creating an unrelated water-navigation predecessor for land pack transport.
ontology_after: Pack Animal Transport depends on animal domestication and basic rope making; navigation remains relevant to maritime and route-planning nodes rather than the immediate pack-load technology.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: early_navigation is no longer a direct prerequisite for pack_animal_transport.
would_reject_if: The node were re-scoped to caravan route navigation systems rather than animal load transport.
A stronger source showed the earliest pack animal use depended specifically on formal navigation technology.
The early_navigation node were broadened away from coastal and open-water navigation into all route finding.
short_reason: Land pack transport is not directly enabled by nautical navigation.
